text:
A Heat Advisory covers Burlington until 8 tonight, so if you're outside this afternoon, that's the plan to build around: shade, water, and short stretches in the sun. We're flirting with triple digits in feel, a high right around 98 with heat index near 105, and there's some wildfire smoke drifting in on top of it, nudging air quality into the moderate range. Take it easy out there.

The bigger story is tonight. A cold front drops storms toward the border through the evening, and the risk here is real, an enhanced severe threat with damaging wind gusts possible, large hail, and a small tornado chance in the northern half of our area. Storms move quickly, so flooding should stay minor, but I'd have a way to get warnings after dark. On the water, winds swing from south to west through the day, waves stay a manageable 1 to 2 feet, but get off the lake well before evening thunder rolls in.

Wednesday clears out nicely behind the front, sunny with a high near 85, though the models disagree some on just how much cooler it gets. I'd plan on comfortable, not chilly.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-14T10:50:00+00:00): None, 75F feels 75F, humidity 50%, wind S 11 mph
ALERTS: Heat Advisory issued July 14 at 2:24AM EDT until July 14 at 8:00PM EDT by NWS Burlington VT
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Mostly sunny, with a high near 98. Heat index values as high as 105. Southwest wind around 13 mph, with gusts as high as 24 mph.
  Tonight: A slight chance of rain showers between 7pm and 8pm, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 8pm and 11pm, then patchy smoke and showers and thunderstorms between 11pm and 2am, then showers and thunderstorms likely. Some of the storms could be severe. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 72. Heat index values as high as 103. West wind 5 to 12 mph, with gusts as high as 24 mph. Chance of precipitation is 90%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.
  Wednesday: A chance of rain showers before 8am. Sunny, with a high near 85. Northwest wind 5 to 15 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.
  Wednesday Night: Mostly clear, with a low around 66. West wind 5 to 12 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-14T06:30:00+00:00):
  1. A Heat Advisory is in effect for Vermont and much of northern New York today, along with some wildfire smoke moving into the region. Some localized fire weather concerns are possible
  2. An Enhanced risk of severe thunderstorms remains for northeastern New York and northern Vermont tonight when significant severe weather and isolated flash flooding is possible. A more typical potential for showers and perhaps some thunderstorms returns for Thursday.
  3. Relatively seasonable weekend and start to next week with shower chances.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 228 AM EDT Tuesday... Smoke has been added to parts of northern New York and Vermont based on wildfire smoke developing across Canada. The potential for high heat and widespread strong to severe thunderstorms, especially along the international border, remains on track.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: South winds 10 to 15 knots, becoming west. Partly sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Tonight: West winds around 10 knots, increasing to 5 to 15 knots. Patchy smoke in the evening. Showers and thunderstorms likely in the evening, then showers and thunderstorms overnight. Some thunderstorms may be severe with damaging winds, large hail, heavy rainfall, and frequent lightning. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Wednesday: Northwest winds 5 to 15 knots. Mostly sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Wednesday Night: Northwest winds 5 to 15 knots, becoming west. Clear. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 76F, level 96.35 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-14: highs [GFS 96, Euro 96, ICON 98] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 22%, Euro 20%, ICON 38%]
  2026-07-15: highs [GFS 88, Euro 82, ICON 85] spread 6F; precip chance [GFS 33%, Euro 56%, ICON 55%]
  2026-07-16: highs [GFS 83, Euro 83, ICON 81] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 40%, Euro 29%, ICON 15%]
AIR: AQI 55 Moderate (PM2.5) — On Tuesday, July 14th,  expect moderate air quality in Vermont, as smoke from Canadian wildfires continues to circulate around the New England region.
SUN: rise 2026-07-14T05:21-04:00, set 2026-07-14T20:35-04:00, UV max 7.2
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 97, low 71: Morning clouds followed by afternoon sun. Highs in the upper 90s and lows in the low 70s.
  high 86, low 64: Mix of sun and clouds. Highs in the mid 80s and lows in the mid 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: A cold front will be moving through our region late Tuesday and overnight into early Wednesday. Thunderstorms will likely fire up along the front in Canada through Tuesday afternoon, likely reaching the International Border Tuesday evening. As a result of the storms, the Storm Prediction Center (SPC) has placed the northern parts of our region under an enhanced risk for severe weather which is a level 3 out of 5 on the severe risk scale. With any severe storms, the greatest risk will be damaging winds. Wind gusts of 70+ mph are possible which means we could be seeing scattered power outages Tuesday evening. There’s also a risk for large hail within any severe storms. Heavy downpours are also expected, but the risk for flooding will be minor as the storms look to move pretty quickly. The SPC also has a 2% and 5% risk for tornadoes in the northern half of our region for Tuesday evening.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-13T22:35:00Z): Intense wind, tornadoes possible Tuesday evening — Northern areas most at risk Intense wind, tornadoes possible Tuesday evening Northern areas most at risk
